definition: A view, standpoint, or way of holding things to be so, used across both neutral and blameworthy doctrinal contexts.
notes: The project keeps view as the default because it is broad enough to govern right view, wrong view, identity view, and clinging-to-views compounds without prejudging the moral status of every occurrence. Viewpoint, opinion, and belief remain controlled alternates for narrower explanatory prose, but translators should not let them displace view in doctrinal formulae or path language. Wrong view is specifically discouraged as a blanket default because diṭṭhi is not automatically blameworthy in every context; the corpus also needs stable handling for sammā-diṭṭhi and for more neutral explanatory use. Doctrine is discouraged because it hardens the term into a system of propositions and weakens the practical, held, and appropriated force that compounds such as diṭṭhupādāna depend on. The OSF glossary confirms view as the house default, while MN 9 and MN 117 support the need to keep the family open enough to cover both right and wrong uses without flattening them together.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and path-factor contexts
In compounds such as sammā-diṭṭhi, micchā-diṭṭhi, sakkāya-diṭṭhi, and diṭṭhupādāna
context_rules.notes: Keep the headword stable unless the compound entry documents an explicit override.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing a held standpoint or angle rather than the formal doctrinal family
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate when the practical sense of taking a position needs to be explicit.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing a narrower personal or disputable claim
context_rules.notes: Use selectively; do not let it become the doctrinal default.

context_rules.context: In prose emphasizing assent or conviction rather than the wider family of view-language
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled explanatory alternate, not the default rendering.

example_phrases.notes: Shows that the headword is not automatically blameworthy.
Shows the same headword in ethical and doctrinal contrast.
Illustrates how the headword extends into appropriation language.

version_notes: Expanded to distinguish neutral and blameworthy uses explicitly and to align the view family with the identity-construction cluster.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping view as the house default across the diṭṭhi family, with viewpoint, opinion, and belief retained as explanatory alternates.
Supports the neutral and positive path-factor use in sammā-diṭṭhi and blocks flattening the whole family into wrong view.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'view' as the default rendering in the entry's governed doctrinal contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not default the headword to 'wrong view', and do not harden it into 'doctrine' when the held, practical, or appropriative sense matters more than a formal system of propositions.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between neutral, path-factor, blameworthy, and appropriative uses of the diṭṭhi family.
